candidate_problems.text: Define explanatory rigidity rho(E) in a way that separates genuine load-bearing constraint from brittle overfitting, since both can make local perturbations fail.
Determine whether small invariant-preserving transformation sets reliably track explanatory quality across domains, including mathematics, physics, and historical explanation.
Construct perturbation tests that distinguish semantic rephrasings from structure-altering substitutions so that T*(E) is not contaminated by surface variation.
Investigate whether high explanatory reach and high rigidity can come apart—for example, explanations with broad scope but many interchangeable micro-realizations.
